data.title: Fitts's Law
data.description: User interface elements are placed where they are easily accessible according to Fitts's Law.
data.technical_spec: Design layout ensures frequently used items are large and center-aligned.

data.title: Hick's Law
data.description: Limits the number of choices presented to users to streamline decision-making.
data.technical_spec: Restricts actions per screen to optimize decision time.

data.title: Aesthetic-Usability Effect
data.description: Designs are made aesthetically pleasing to enhance perceived usability.
data.technical_spec: Emphasizes visual hierarchy and crisp image resolutions.

data.title: Miller's Law
data.description: Adapts information chunks to optimize memory load on users.
data.technical_spec: Breaks down content into digestible pieces with logical categorization.

data.title: Jakob's Law
data.description: Follows established design paradigms to meet user expectations.
data.technical_spec: Adoption of conventional navigation and interaction patterns.
